Title: The synthesis of xyloglucan, an abundant plant cell wall polysaccharide, requires CSLC function
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QxxRW motif; multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked glucan backbone used as the core scaffold of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural component of primary cell walls; contributes to wall organization and growth-related mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal xyloglucan accumulation; contributes redundantly to xyloglucan biosynthesis, and loss in higher-order mutants reduces xyloglucan levels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed; highly expressed in root hairs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed; high in root hairs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA loss-of-function mutant analysis in higher-order combinations; Driselase digestion with isoprimeverose quantification; glycan array with LM15 antibody; OLIMP MALDI-TOF; complementation of cslc quintuple mutant with CSLC4pro::CSLC4
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: standard growth conditions; analyses in etiolated hypocotyls, root hairs, and pollen-related tissues
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate xyloglucan content and cell wall architecture in plant biomass engineering; likely requires multiplex editing or stacking because of functional redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order loss causes smaller rosettes, shorter inflorescence stems, stem bending when cslc4 allele is present, and contributes to root hair defects with cslc12
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutants show near-normal xyloglucan; redundancy with other CSLC family members. Previous literature cited indicates CSLC4 forms Golgi-localized complexes with XXT proteins, but this interaction was not experimentally tested in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLC4 is one of five Arabidopsis CSLC genes required redundantly for xyloglucan backbone synthesis. 2. Single cslc4 mutants retain near-normal xyloglucan, but higher-order mutants containing cslc4 show reduced xyloglucan and stem-bending phenotypes. 3. CSLC4 expression can complement the cslc quintuple mutant, restoring isoprimeverose levels and root hair length.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QxxRW motif; multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked glucan backbone used as the core scaffold of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural component of primary cell walls; contributes to wall organization and growth-related mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redundantly for xyloglucan biosynthesis; complementation with CSLC5 restores xyloglucan in the cslc quintuple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA loss-of-function mutant combinations; Driselase/isoprimeverose quantification; complementation of cslc quintuple mutant with CSLC4pro::CSLC5
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering xyloglucan deposition in seed-related or other tissues, but family redundancy means single-gene manipulation may be insufficient
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious phenotype in single mutant; higher-order mutants show reduced growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This paper supports biochemical redundancy with other CSLC proteins despite distinct expression pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLC5 is a member of the Arabidopsis CSLC family contributing to xyloglucan backbone synthesis. 2. Its native expression is enriched in developing seeds. 3. CSLC5 expression complements the cslc quintuple mutant, restoring isoprimeverose and root hair growth, supporting glucan synthase activity.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QxxRW motif; multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked glucan backbone used as the core scaffold of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural component of primary cell walls; contributes to wall organization and growth-related mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redundantly for xyloglucan biosynthesis; CSLC6 strongly complements the cslc quintuple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA higher-order mutant analysis; Driselase/isoprimeverose quantification; complementation of cslc quintuple mutant with CSLC4pro::CSLC6
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for modulating wall xyloglucan while considering reproductive tissue expression and redundancy
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order cslc mutants show growth defects; single mutant near normal
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CSLC6 complementation restored xyloglucan and root hair length to near or above wild-type levels in some lines.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLC6 contributes redundantly to xyloglucan backbone synthesis. 2. It is preferentially expressed in pollen. 3. CSLC6 robustly complements the quintuple mutant, supporting that it encodes a xyloglucan glucan synthase.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QxxRW motif; multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked glucan backbone used as the core scaffold of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural component of primary cell walls; contributes to wall organization and growth-related mechanics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redundantly for xyloglucan biosynthesis; CSLC8 can partially complement the cslc quintuple mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed at lower levels than CSLC4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed at relatively low level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA higher-order mutant analysis; Driselase/isoprimeverose quantification; complementation of cslc quintuple mutant with 35Spro::CSLC8
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering xyloglucan synthesis, though complementation was weaker than some other CSLC members under tested constructs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order mutant combinations reduce growth; single mutant near normal
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Complementation by CSLC8 was partial and variable across lines.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLC8 is a redundant xyloglucan backbone synthase family member. 2. Its endogenous expression is broad but lower than CSLC4. 3. Constitutive CSLC8 expression partially restores xyloglucan and root hair length in the cslc quintuple mutant.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like C family; GT2 glycosyltransferase; D,D,D,QxxRW motif; multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes the β-1,4-linked glucan backbone used as the core scaffold of xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: structural component of primary cell walls; contributes to wall organization and growth-related mechanics; implicated in reproductive and root hair development through tissue-specific wall function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required redundantly for xyloglucan biosynthesis; loss with other CSLC genes contributes to shorter root hairs and reduced pollen tube formation, while complementation restores xyloglucan and partially restores root hair length
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in pollen grains and root hairs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in pollen grains and root hairs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA higher-order mutant analysis; phenotype analysis in root hairs and pollen tube formation; Driselase/isoprimeverose quantification; complementation of cslc quintuple mutant with 35Spro::CSLC12
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying tissue-specific xyloglucan functions in roots and reproduction, but manipulation may affect fertility-related processes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order mutants containing cslc12 show shorter root hairs and reduced pollen tube formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with CSLC4, CSLC12 appears particularly important for root hair growth and pollen–pistil interactions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CSLC12 is a redundant xyloglucan glucan synthase with tissue-enriched expression in pollen and root hairs. 2. Mutants carrying cslc12 in higher-order backgrounds show root hair and pollen-related defects. 3. Constitutive CSLC12 expression complements the quintuple mutant, restoring xyloglucan accumulation and improving root hair growth.
